pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce Zod as a TypeScript-first schema validation library, explaining its core philosophy ('parse, don't validate'), key features, and position in the ecosystem

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Guide users through installation, basic setup, and first schema creation

pages.title: Installation
pages.purpose: Explain installation via npm/yarn/bun, discuss package exports (CJS/ESM/Deno), and TypeScript configuration requirements

pages.title: Basic Usage
pages.purpose: Demonstrate simple schema definition, parsing, and type inference with minimal examples

pages.title: Core Concepts
pages.purpose: Explain fundamental concepts: schemas as validators and type definitions, the parsing pipeline, and type inference

pages.title: Schemas and Validation
pages.purpose: Explain what schemas are, how they define both runtime validation and compile-time types, and the immutability principle

pages.title: Parsing Methods
pages.purpose: Document parse(), safeParse(), parseAsync(), and safeParseAsync(), explaining when to use each and their return types

pages.title: Type Inference
pages.purpose: Explain z.infer, z.input, z.output, and how Zod enables deriving TypeScript types from runtime schemas

pages.title: Primitive Types
pages.purpose: Document all primitive schema types (string, number, boolean, date, bigint, symbol, etc.) and their validators

pages.title: String Schemas
pages.purpose: Detail string validation methods: min, max, length, email, url, uuid, datetime, regex, and string transformations (trim, toLowerCase, etc.)

pages.title: Number and BigInt Schemas
pages.purpose: Document numeric validation: min, max, int, positive, negative, multipleOf, finite, safe, and BigInt-specific methods

pages.title: Date and Other Primitives
pages.purpose: Cover date schemas, boolean, symbol, undefined, null, void, and special types (any, unknown, never)

pages.title: Object Schemas
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to object schemas: definition, shape access, and unknown key handling strategies

pages.title: Defining Object Schemas
pages.purpose: Explain z.object(), .shape property, and basic object validation with nested schemas

pages.title: Unknown Keys Handling
pages.purpose: Document .strict(), .passthrough(), .strip(), .catchall() and their effects on validation and type inference

pages.title: Object Manipulation Methods
pages.purpose: Detail .extend(), .merge(), .pick(), .omit(), .partial(), .deepPartial(), .required(), and .keyof()

pages.title: Collection Schemas
pages.purpose: Document schemas for collections: arrays, tuples, records, maps, and sets

pages.title: Arrays
pages.purpose: Explain z.array(), .element, .min/.max/.length, .nonempty, and array-specific validation

pages.title: Tuples
pages.purpose: Document z.tuple(), fixed-length validation, .rest() for variadic tuples, and tuple type inference

pages.title: Records, Maps, and Sets
pages.purpose: Explain z.record() for Record<K,V> types, z.map() and z.set() for native JavaScript collections

pages.title: Type Composition
pages.purpose: Document how to compose schemas using unions, intersections, enums, and literals

pages.title: Unions
pages.purpose: Explain z.union(), .or() method, discriminated unions, and union type inference

pages.title: Intersections
pages.purpose: Document z.intersection() and .and() method for combining schemas with AND logic

pages.title: Enums and Literals
pages.purpose: Explain z.enum(), z.nativeEnum() for TypeScript enums, and z.literal() for single-value schemas

pages.title: Schema Modifiers
pages.purpose: Document wrapper schemas that modify behavior: optional, nullable, default, and branded types

pages.title: Optional and Nullable
pages.purpose: Explain .optional(), .nullable(), .nullish(), and their effects on type inference and validation

pages.title: Default Values
pages.purpose: Document .default() for static and dynamic defaults, explaining when defaults are applied in the pipeline

pages.title: Branded Types and Readonly
pages.purpose: Explain .brand() for nominal typing and .readonly() for immutable type inference

pages.title: Transformations and Refinements
pages.purpose: Document how to add custom validation and data transformation logic to schemas

pages.title: Refinements
pages.purpose: Explain .refine() and .superRefine() for custom validation, including ctx.addIssue and async refinements

pages.title: Transformations
pages.purpose: Document .transform() for data modification, explaining input/output type duality, sync/async transforms, and z.NEVER

pages.title: Preprocessing and Pipelines
pages.purpose: Explain z.preprocess() for pre-validation transformations and .pipe() for chaining schemas

pages.title: Coercion
pages.purpose: Document z.coerce.string(), z.coerce.number(), etc. for automatic type conversion before validation

pages.title: Error Handling
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to Zod's error system: ZodError, ZodIssue, error maps, and error formatting

pages.title: ZodError and ZodIssue
pages.purpose: Explain the structure of ZodError, the ZodIssue discriminated union, and ZodIssueCode types

pages.title: Error Maps and Custom Messages
pages.purpose: Document the error map priority chain (default → global → schema → contextual) and z.setErrorMap()

pages.title: Error Formatting
pages.purpose: Explain .format() for nested errors, .flatten() for flat field errors, and type inference utilities

pages.title: Advanced Schema Types
pages.purpose: Document specialized schema types: functions, promises, instanceof, and custom schemas

pages.title: Function Schemas
pages.purpose: Explain z.function(), .args(), .returns(), .implement(), and function schema validation

pages.title: Promise Schemas
pages.purpose: Document z.promise() and .promise() method, explaining async validation and two-phase parsing

pages.title: Instance Validation and Custom Schemas
pages.purpose: Explain z.instanceof() for class instances and z.custom() for defining schemas for custom TypeScript types

pages.title: Internal Architecture
pages.purpose: Deep dive into Zod's implementation: parsing pipeline, type system, and error generation

pages.title: Parse Pipeline and Context
pages.purpose: Explain ParseContext, ParseStatus, ParseInput, and the complete parsing lifecycle from input to result

pages.title: ZodType Base Class and Subclasses
pages.purpose: Document the ZodType abstract base class, _parse method signature, and how specific types extend it

pages.title: Issue Generation and Error Map Resolution
pages.purpose: Explain makeIssue, addIssueToContext, and the error map priority resolution chain

pages.title: Type Utilities and Helpers
pages.purpose: Document util and objectUtil namespaces, getParsedType, and ZodParsedType enumeration

pages.title: Project Structure and Build System
pages.purpose: Explain repository organization, build configuration, and distribution strategy

pages.title: Repository Structure
pages.purpose: Document directory layout: src/, lib/, deno/lib/, test organization, and configuration files

pages.title: Build Targets and Compilation
pages.purpose: Explain build process: TypeScript compilation to CJS/ESM, Rollup bundling, and Deno-specific builds

pages.title: Package Exports and Module Formats
pages.purpose: Document package.json exports field, module/main/types configuration, and how consumers import Zod

pages.title: Development Workflow
pages.purpose: Guide contributors through development setup, testing, and code quality tools

pages.title: Testing Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Document test setup (Jest, Vitest, Deno), running tests, and test organization patterns

pages.title: Code Quality Tools
pages.purpose: Explain ESLint configuration, Prettier setup, Husky pre-commit hooks, and lint-staged

pages.title: Local Development Scripts
pages.purpose: Document yarn scripts: build, test, lint, playground, and their usage during development

pages.title: CI/CD and Release Process
pages.purpose: Explain automated testing, release workflows, and publication to npm

pages.title: Continuous Integration
pages.purpose: Document test.yml workflow: matrix testing across Node.js/TypeScript versions, Deno testing, and linting

pages.title: Release Workflows
pages.purpose: Explain stable release workflow (package.json triggers) vs canary releases (source change triggers)

pages.title: Multi-Platform Distribution
pages.purpose: Document npm publication, Deno repository synchronization, and GitHub releases with changelogs

pages.title: Version History and Migration
pages.purpose: Document major version changes, breaking changes, and migration guides based on CHANGELOG

pages.title: Zod 3.x Evolution
pages.purpose: Summarize key changes across Zod 3.x versions: new features, API improvements, and architectural changes

pages.title: Zod 4 Development
pages.purpose: Document Zod 4 roadmap, Clerk fellowship, planned breaking changes, and new features
